import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from myapp.client import ApiClient

@pytest.fixture
def mock_http():
    with patch("myapp.client.requests.get") as mock_get, \
         patch("myapp.client.requests.post") as mock_post:
        yield {"get": mock_get, "post": mock_post}

def test_fetch_user(mock_http):
    mock_response = MagicMock()
    mock_response.status_code = 200
    mock_response.json.return_value = {"id": 1, "name": "Alice"}
    mock_http["get"].return_value = mock_response

    client = ApiClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
    response = client.get("/users/1")
    assert response["id"] == 1
    assert "name" in response

def test_create_post(mock_http):
    mock_response = MagicMock()
    mock_response.status_code = 201
    mock_response.json.return_value = {"id": 101}
    mock_http["post"].return_value = mock_response

    client = ApiClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
    result = client.create_post({"title": "test", "body": "content"})
    assert result["id"] == 101

def test_health_check(mock_http):
    mock_response = MagicMock()
    mock_response.status_code = 200
    mock_http["get"].return_value = mock_response

    client = ApiClient(base_url="https://api.example.com")
    assert client.health_check() is True
